tldr: Five items shipped this run (#21, #22, #23, #26, #27). #21 (Google Business Profile services audit, Tier 1) was completed manually off-site by the owner. #22 (neck-pain condition page) and #23 (back-pain condition page) shipped via PRs #63 and #64 — both follow the existing condition-page pattern with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema. #26 (llms.txt alternate links in HTML head) shipped via PR #62. #27 (Physician → IndividualPhysician with practicesAt) shipped via PR #65. That clears the backlog of every Tier-1 item — only #17 (dry-needling sub-page) remains in Tier 1, and PR #61 reframed its scope from a parent-page rewrite to a sibling sub-page (mirror of /services/acupuncture/fertility/).

Three new items added in this same update (#28, #29, #30) to keep the punch-list at meaningful coverage: #28 (rewrite image alt text — descriptive not titular, addresses G14), #29 (in-content Hillmorton/Wigram/Addington signals — addresses new gap G22), #30 (cross-link condition pages and surface them from osteopathy service intro + homepage — addresses new gap G23). All three are Tier 2, S effort, derived from explicit signals in the 2026-05-08 SERP run (Hillmorton SERP regressed to UK results; condition pages 3 weeks post-ship still aren't surfacing; image-grounded retrieval gaining weight in 2026 GEO guides).

Backlog after this run: 10 items pending (#12, #13, #14, #15, #16, #17, #25, #28, #29, #30). Tier 1 → just #17. Tier 2 → #12, #13, #25, #28, #29, #30. Tier 3 → #14, #15, #16. Closed gaps: G13 (llms.txt head link), G20 (GBP services), G21 (IndividualPhysician schema). Narrowed gap: G7 (only ACC landing remains under #25). New gaps: G22 (no in-content suburb signal), G23 (condition pages not discoverable from elsewhere on site). Search visibility: priorities and pool unchanged from 2026-05-08; no new SERP run this update.
alreadyShipped: llms.txt and llms-full.txt auto-generated from site data
Explicit AI-crawler allow-list in robots.txt (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot-Extended, CCBot, anthropic-ai)
MedicalClinic + MedicalBusiness JSON-LD in base template with address, hours, priceRange, offer catalog
FAQPage JSON-LD on the homepage
Canonical URLs, OpenGraph, Twitter Card tags on every page
Auto-generated sitemap with lastmod
AggregateRating + Review schema (ratingValue 4.9, reviewCount 65, three real testimonials) nested under MedicalBusiness
Physician JSON-LD per team profile with sameAs to professional registers and visible 'Registered with …' block
Per-service MedicalTherapy / Service schema with provider, areaServed, hasOfferCatalog of specialties, and mainEntityOfPage
FAQPage JSON-LD on every service page (driven by faq arrays in services.json)
BreadcrumbList JSON-LD + visible breadcrumb strip on non-home pages
Visible 'Last updated' block on service and team pages, with dateModified in WebPage schema
sameAs block on clinic JSON-LD (Google Business Profile etc.)
Expanded llms.txt — Conditions-treated, Pricing-&-ACC, Common-questions sections
Homepage H1 reframed to 'Osteopathy, Acupuncture & Herbal Medicine in Christchurch'; practitioners section demoted to H2

/conditions/sciatica/, /conditions/headaches-migraines/, /conditions/pregnancy-back-pain/, /conditions/sports-injury-recovery/ — four condition Q→A pages with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, plus a What We Treat nav dropdown
ProfilePage + MedicalWebPage @graph schema on every team profile (alongside the Physician node), so each practitioner URL is its own retrievable AI-search entity
Hero subtitle and practitioners intro reframed to evidence-informed language (no longer says "holistic"); each line is now distinct rather than restating the H1
"Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs" — site-wide footer block + extended llms.txt Area-served line
Treats: chips on every team profile (in a What I Treat card), with Nina's row carrying a clickable pink-tinted pill linking to the new fertility page
/services/acupuncture/fertility/ sub-page — MedicalTherapy schema linking to the parent acupuncture #service, FAQPage on IVF timing / ACC status / Nina's credentials, hero + 3 specialty images
Site-wide sweep removing 'GP referral' from patient-facing copy (per the CLAUDE.md copy rule); condition CTA banners simplified to heading + booking button only

Google Business Profile services audit completed manually (item #21) — owner added Osteopathy, Medical acupuncture, Dry needling, Neck pain treatment, Headache & migraine treatment, Sports injury treatment, Pregnancy back pain, Paediatric osteopathy, Herbal medicine to GBP service list

/conditions/neck-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema, mirroring the four item-#10 pages; covers acute vs chronic, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links back to the osteopathy service (item #22, PR #63)

/conditions/back-pain/ — Q→A page with MedicalCondition + FAQPage schema; covers acute vs chronic, sciatica overlap, when to seek urgent care, ACC eligibility, links to /conditions/sciatica/ and the osteopathy service (item #23, PR #64)

<link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown" href="/llms.txt"> + the same for /llms-full.txt added in the HTML head — signals AI crawlers that a Markdown summary exists alongside the HTML (item #26, PR #62)

Physician schema upgraded to Schema.org v30 IndividualPhysician on every team profile, with practicesAt: { @id: '#clinic' } linking each practitioner to the MedicalClinic node — sharpens entity disambiguation for ChatGPT / Perplexity / AI Overviews (item #27, PR #65)

gaps.title: No ACC landing page
gaps.why: Neck-pain and back-pain condition pages now ship (items #22, #23 closed). The remaining piece of G7 is a single indexable /acc/ URL — osteo.co.nz still owns 'osteopath acc christchurch' and we have ACC mentions scattered across the site but no canonical landing. Scoped under item #25.

gaps.title: No blog / editorial content
gaps.why: Freshness cliff at ~3 months. April-2026 industry data: 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within 30 days, 50% of Perplexity citations come from content less than 13 weeks old. osteopath-christchurch.com is winning headache/migraine/cranial/baby-osteo queries on blog-post velocity — 1 short post per condition is enough to claim those niches. MK Osteopathy is doing the same for TMJ.

gaps.title: Image alt text could be richer
gaps.why: AI-image-grounded models benefit from descriptive, not titular, alt text. Reinforced by 2026 GEO guides emphasising image-grounded retrieval — Tier-3 framing may understate the urgency.

gaps.title: No off-site citation strategy
gaps.why: Reddit appears in ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations (May 2026 CMSWire/Conductor data) — still the single most influential third-party source across all major LLMs. Item #15 still applies.

gaps.title: No Western-framed dry-needling landing URL
gaps.why: The gap isn't that the acupuncture page leads with TCM (that's correct framing for our existing patient base), it's that we have NO indexable URL targeting the Western-medical / dry-needling intent at all. 'dry needling christchurch' is 100% physio-owned and the Halswell+modality SERP is genuinely empty of NZ content. Item #17 now adds a sibling sub-page (mirror of /services/acupuncture/fertility/) rather than reframing the parent page.

gaps.title: No in-content suburb signals beyond the footer line
gaps.why: We have a single footer line ('Serving Hillmorton, Halswell, Hoon Hay, Cashmere, Addington, Riccarton, Sydenham, Wigram & surrounding Christchurch suburbs') and the same in llms.txt — but no service or service-intro paragraph actually mentions any suburb in flowing copy. SERP evidence (2026-05-08): 'osteopath hillmorton' regressed to all-UK results (Google found nothing NZ-relevant for our actual location); 'osteopath wigram' is uncontested NZ-side; 'osteopath addington' is contested only by Performance Plus. A single sentence per service intro naming Hillmorton + the closest suburbs would give Google something local to surface. Addressed by item #29.

gaps.title: Condition pages exist but aren't discoverable from elsewhere on the site
gaps.why: Four condition pages from item #10 (sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery) are 3 weeks post-ship and still don't surface for their target queries. The 'What We Treat' nav dropdown is the only on-site link path to them — they're not referenced from the osteopathy service intro, not cross-linked between related conditions, and not surfaced on the homepage. Internal link surfaces pass relevance signals to Google and accelerate indexing. Same indexing-lag risk now applies to the new neck-pain and back-pain pages. Addressed by item #30.

items.title: Add speakable blocks on service intros
items.body: Add schema.org speakable specification on service intro paragraphs for voice assistants / Gemini Live.

items.title: Auto-fetch Google Business Profile reviews at build time
items.body: So reviewCount / ratingValue stay current without manual edits. Build script hits Places API with a 24h cache. Reinforced this week: post-March-2026 Google update weights review recency more heavily than raw count.

items.title: Start a blog (2 posts/month)
items.body: Short evidence-based articles per condition. Each post: author byline with Physician schema, datePublished, dateModified, 3–5 FAQs. Ahrefs analysis of 17M AI citations (April 2026) found AI-cited content is 25.7% fresher than traditional Google organic results — 76.4% of ChatGPT's top-cited pages were updated within the last 30 days. Freshness compounds. Confirmed again 2026-05-08: osteopath-christchurch.com owns headaches/cranial/baby/teeth-grinding queries on the strength of one short blog post per condition; MK Osteopathy claimed the TMJ slot the same way.

items.title: Seed Reddit / community brand mentions
items.body: Genuine, non-spammy answers on r/newzealand / r/chch / r/AskNZ threads about osteopathy, ACC claims, back pain. Reddit is now ~47% of Perplexity citations and ~5% of ChatGPT citations across major AI platforms (May 2026 Conductor / CMSWire data). 90/10 rule: 90% non-promotional, 2–4 weeks of karma-building before any mention of Meridian.

items.title: Encourage monthly Google reviews nudge
items.body: Post-consultation email nudge via Resend (already wired). AI engines prefer reviews under 12 months old. Reinforced 2026-05-02: Google's March 2026 core update now weights review recency AND owner response rate more heavily than raw count. Healthcare best-practice = at least 1 new review/week.

items.title: Add /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ sub-page (Western framing) — keep main acupuncture page TCM-first
items.body: REVISED 2026-05-10 per owner feedback: the original framing (reframe the main acupuncture page to lead with Western medical / dry needling) would alienate existing TCM patients who book Nina specifically for Traditional Chinese Medicine. Instead, mirror the pattern already shipped at /services/acupuncture/fertility/: keep the parent /services/medical-acupuncture/ page TCM-first as it is today, and add a sibling sub-page /services/acupuncture/dry-needling/ that leads with 'Medical acupuncture / dry needling — Western evidence-based technique for trigger-point and musculoskeletal pain'. Two indexable URLs, each surfacing for its own intent, no compromise on TCM identity. Sub-page should: (a) name Nina's Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture) in the first paragraph — exact credential ChatGPT told users to look for; (b) explicitly mention Halswell so Google has local content to surface (the Halswell+modality SERP is genuinely empty of NZ content as of 2026-05-08); (c) carry a MedicalTherapy schema linking back to the parent acupuncture #service; (d) include a FAQPage block on dry-needling-vs-acupuncture, ACC status, and what to expect. Why this is now the better shape: the existing fertility sub-page already proves the pattern works in our build, and 'dry needling christchurch' is still 100% physio-owned (Muscle People, Fixed Physio, Physiosouth, Physio NZ, Koru Therapies) so a dedicated landing URL is the smallest defensible move. Effort stays S — small templated page mirroring the fertility sub-page.

items.title: Not ranking for 'osteopath acc christchurch' — osteo.co.nz owns it
items.body: osteo.co.nz has a dedicated ACC landing page that wins this query (confirmed again 2026-05-08). We already have the ACC pricing row in services.json and scattered ACC mentions, but no single indexable /acc/ URL. Build a short page (~500 words) explaining ACC-funded treatment at Meridian, the current ACC rate, no referral required, how to lodge a claim, and link out to both service pages and a booking CTA. Low-effort content fix.

items.title: Audit and rewrite image alt text — descriptive, not titular
items.body: G14 has been in the gaps list since the first audit but had no matching item. Most alt text on the site is titular ('Nina Hu', 'hero-sciatica.jpg') rather than descriptive. 2026 GEO guides specifically call out image-grounded retrieval gaining weight in multimodal LLM retrieval — descriptive alt text helps Gemini, ChatGPT (with vision) and Perplexity understand and cite the image, not just the surrounding HTML. Audit every in the site, skip decorative SVG icons (use alt=''), and rewrite content alts to describe what's visible plus relevant clinical context. Examples: team photos → 'Osteopath Nina Hu seated at her treatment table in the Hillmorton clinic'; condition hero → 'Anatomical illustration of the sciatic nerve travelling from the lower spine down the back of the leg'; service hero → 'Osteopath performing gentle cervical mobilisation on a seated patient'. Effort small (one editor pass per file).

items.title: Add Hillmorton / Wigram / Addington signals beyond the footer line
items.body: We have a single site-wide footer catchment line and the matching llms.txt area-served line, but no service intro actually names a suburb in flowing copy. SERP evidence 2026-05-08: 'osteopath hillmorton' (our actual location) regressed to all-UK results because Google sees no NZ-relevant content beyond the footer; 'osteopath wigram' is fully uncontested NZ-side; 'osteopath addington' is only partially contested by Performance Plus. Smallest viable fix: add one in-content sentence per service intro naming Hillmorton + the closest suburbs, e.g. 'Our Hillmorton clinic serves patients across Halswell, Wigram, Addington, Cashmere and surrounding Christchurch suburbs.' If a longer play is wanted later, a small /find-us/ page with directions, parking and a paragraph naming each surrounding suburb gives more substance — but the in-content sentence is the high-leverage low-effort start. Tier 2, S effort.

items.title: Cross-link condition pages and surface them from osteopathy intro + homepage
items.body: Four condition pages from item #10 (sciatica, headaches-migraines, pregnancy-back-pain, sports-injury-recovery) are 3 weeks post-ship and still don't surface for their target queries. Same indexing-lag risk now applies to the just-shipped neck-pain and back-pain. Likely root cause: the 'What We Treat' nav dropdown is the only on-site link path to them. Three small additions: (a) a 'Conditions we commonly treat' block on the osteopathy service page intro that links to each condition page; (b) cross-links between related condition pages — neck-pain ↔ headaches-migraines (both upper-quadrant), sciatica ↔ back-pain (overlap), pregnancy-back-pain → back-pain, sports-injury-recovery → relevant body areas; (c) a small 'Common conditions we treat' surface on the homepage with 2-3 of the highest-intent condition links. Internal link surfaces pass relevance signals and accelerate indexing. Tier 2, S effort.
